title: Flatpack manifest
description: Schema for the inline FLATPACK:MANIFEST JSON block carried by every Flatpack. Documentation only — Flatpacks do not load or validate against this schema at runtime. See SPEC.md §6.1.

artifactType.description: Canonical shape — one of the templates the tool was based on, or a new shape name.

version.description: Semver. Matches APP_META.version. Bumped together with the storage key when state shape changes.

scope.description: personal = one user / one device (default). shared-read-only = still a personal artifact; the file may be sent to others (like a PDF) but there is no shared state, no collaboration, no central authority, no expectation of synchronised data across recipients. Anything more is a promotion event.

persistence.description: Disclosed verbatim in the privacy banner.

network.description: none = no calls. user-supplied-endpoint = the tool's purpose is to call an endpoint the user pastes in at runtime. No embedded URLs.

minimumBrowser.description: Floor for each major engine the file is known to require. Defaults if omitted: Chrome 100, Safari 15.4, Firefox 98, Edge 100.

entities.description: The user-meaningful records the tool manipulates. The promotion plan uses these to propose database models.

type.description: Free-form type label. Conventional: string, number, date, boolean, enum, text, list, list[string], ref.

validations.description: Plain-English rules. Each item is a single rule. The promotion agent converts these to backend + frontend validation. Kept for human readability; the structured form for machine verification is `validation_predicates`.

validation_predicates.description: Optional structured form of `validations`. Each predicate names a field, a constraint, and (for parametric constraints) a value. A promotion-time verifier on the receiving side can resolve these against the destination's field declarations directly, sidestepping the text-matching false-positives that the plain `validations` array suffers from. See https://github.com/ConceptPending/baseplate/issues/33 for the motivation.

field.description: Name of the field on the relevant entity (must appear in `entities[].fields[].name`).

constraint.description: The constraint kind. Conventional vocabulary: required, gt, gte, lt, lte, min_length, max_length, one_of, unique, format, not_in_future. Free-text — extend as new shapes appear.

value.description: Optional parameter for the constraint. e.g. for `gt` the threshold; for `one_of` the list; for `format` the format name.

description.description: Plain-English rendering of the predicate. Matches one of the strings in `validations` when present.

exports.description: Output formats the tool produces. Conventional values: clean_csv, errors_csv, json, csv, markdown, markdown_clipboard, summary_clipboard, summary_print, print_pdf.

imports.description: Input formats the tool accepts (if any beyond direct input).

promotionSignals.description: Concrete events that, if any of them happens, mean the tool has stopped being personal and should be promoted into Baseplate.

archetype.description: Free-text label for the whole-app archetype this tool represents. NOT a Baseplate recipe — Baseplate recipes are modifications layered on a base app. The promotion agent translates archetype → recipe set. Conventional values used in this repo: internal-tool, import-validate-store, decision-log, case-workspace, workflow-with-checklist, branching-questionnaire.
